justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) console.log flagged as unstructured and insufficient — 'raw console.log with string concatenation, unsearchable in any log aggregator'; (2) missing /health endpoint explicitly flagged — 'no health endpoint' in monitoring_coverage and listed as must-have #3; (3) missing metrics flagged comprehensively — no sync duration, no items_synced counter, no error rate, no last_successful_sync gauge; (4) missing timeout on outbound API call flagged as reliability risk — 'No timeout, no retry, no circuit breaker. A slow or unresponsive API will hang the sync indefinitely'; (5) silent failure on API outage flagged — 'stale data persists silently, no mechanism to surface staleness'; (6) verdict is NOT READY with 6 must-fix items enumerated; (7) recommendations are highly actionable and specific — exact timeout values, structured log format example, specific metric names.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — identified all 5 required gaps plus additional valid ones (overlap/concurrency, partial writes, schema validation)
Clear must-have vs nice-to-have distinction: timeout, health, structured logs, metrics are blockers; circuit breaker, schema validation are next-iteration
Recommendations include concrete values: '10s timeout', '3x exponential backoff', specific structured log JSON format
The '3am Test' narrative effectively communicates the operational impact of the gaps to non-technical stakeholders
Escalations correctly identify zero observability and missing timeout as ship-blockers routed to Coach K
No fabricated gaps — all findings are grounded in what the scenario describes

Minor: retry with backoff listed as must-have #2 ship-blocker, but the rubric treats retry as part of the 'silent failure' gap rather than a standalone must-fix. Not a scoring issue but slightly inflates the must-have list beyond what the rubric strictly requires
